metadata.study: A Comparative Analysis of Code Quality Generated by ChatGPT (GPT-4o), Claude (3.5 Sonnet), and GitHub Copilot Using Software Engineering Metrics
metadata.selection_protocol: best-of-3 runs per task per model (highest unit-test pass rate; first session on tie)

tasks.description: Check whether a given integer is a prime number.
tasks.output_specification: Boolean True if n is prime, False otherwise
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function to check whether a given integer is a prime number. The function must accept a single non-negative integer n and return True if n is prime and False otherwise. Do not include explanations; provide only the code.

tasks.description: Reverse a string without using built-in reverse methods.
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function to reverse a string without using built-in reverse methods (do not use slicing [::-1] or reversed()). The function must accept a single string s and return the reversed string. Do not include explanations; provide only the code.

GitHub_Copilot.code:
def reverse_string(s):
    reversed_str = ''
    index = len(s) - 1
    while index >= 0:
        reversed_str += s[index]
        index -= 1
    return reversed_str

tasks.description: Convert temperature values across Celsius, Fahrenheit, and Kelvin.
tasks.input_specification: A float value and two strings: source_unit and target_unit ('C', 'F', or 'K')
tasks.output_specification: A float representing the converted temperature, rounded to 2 decimal places
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function to convert temperature values across Celsius, Fahrenheit, and Kelvin. The function must accept a float value and two strings source_unit and target_unit (each being 'C', 'F', or 'K') and return a float rounded to 2 decimal places. Do not include explanations; provide only the code.

tasks.description: Implement binary search on a sorted list.
tasks.input_specification: A sorted list of integers arr and a target integer
tasks.output_specification: The index of the target in arr, or -1 if not found
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function to implement binary search on a sorted list. The function must accept a sorted list of integers arr and a target integer, and return the index of the target in arr, or -1 if not found. Do not include explanations; provide only the code.

tasks.description: Parse a CSV file and aggregate numeric data by group.
tasks.input_specification: A file path (str) to a CSV with columns 'group' (str) and 'value' (float)
tasks.output_specification: A dict mapping each group name to its summed value, rounded to 2 decimal places
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function to parse a CSV file and aggregate numeric data by group. The function must accept a file path string to a CSV file with columns 'group' (string) and 'value' (float), and return a dict mapping each group name to its total summed value rounded to 2 decimal places. Do not include explanations; provide only the code.

tasks.description: Implement a stack data structure using a linked list.
tasks.input_specification: No constructor arguments; stack supports push(value), pop(), peek(), is_empty(), size()
tasks.output_specification: A Stack class with the described interface
tasks.prompt: Write a Python class to implement a stack data structure using a linked list (not Python's built-in list). The class must support push(value), pop(), peek(), is_empty(), and size() methods. pop() and peek() should raise IndexError on empty stack. Do not include explanations; provide only the code.

tasks.description: Implement breadth-first search (BFS) on an adjacency list graph.
tasks.input_specification: A dict graph representing an adjacency list and a start node (any hashable type)
tasks.output_specification: A list of nodes in BFS traversal order starting from start
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function to perform breadth-first search (BFS) on a graph represented as an adjacency list. The function must accept a dict graph and a start node and return a list of nodes in BFS traversal order. Do not include explanations; provide only the code.

ChatGPT.code:
from collections import deque

def bfs(graph, start):
    visited = set()
    queue = deque([start])
    result = []
    visited.add(start)
    while queue:
        node = queue.popleft()
        result.append(node)
        for neighbor in graph.get(node, []):
            if neighbor not in visited:
                visited.add(neighbor)
                queue.append(neighbor)
    return result

GitHub_Copilot.code:
from collections import deque

def bfs(graph, start):
    visited = []
    queue = deque()
    queue.append(start)
    seen = set()
    seen.add(start)
    while queue:
        current = queue.popleft()
        visited.append(current)
        for neighbor in graph.get(current, []):
            if neighbor not in seen:
                seen.add(neighbor)
                queue.append(neighbor)
    return visited

tasks.description: Implement merge sort and document its time complexity.
tasks.input_specification: A list of comparable elements arr
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function to implement merge sort. The function must accept a list of comparable elements arr and return a new sorted list in ascending order. Include docstring comments documenting the time complexity. Do not include explanations outside the code; provide only the code.

ChatGPT.code:
def merge_sort(arr):
    """
    Sort a list using the merge sort algorithm.
    Time complexity: O(n log n) in all cases.
    Space complexity: O(n) auxiliary space.
    """
    if len(arr) <= 1:
        return arr
    mid = len(arr) // 2
    left = merge_sort(arr[:mid])
    right = merge_sort(arr[mid:])
    return _merge(left, right)

def _merge(left, right):
    result = []
    i = j = 0
    while i < len(left) and j < len(right):
        if left[i] <= right[j]:
            result.append(left[i])
            i += 1
        else:
            result.append(right[j])
            j += 1
    result.extend(left[i:])
    result.extend(right[j:])
    return result

tasks.description: Design an inventory management class with full CRUD operations.
tasks.input_specification: No constructor arguments; items have name (str), quantity (int), price (float)
tasks.output_specification: An InventoryManager class with add_item, get_item, update_item, delete_item, list_items methods
tasks.prompt: Write a Python class to implement an inventory management system with full CRUD operations. The class InventoryManager must support: add_item(name, quantity, price), get_item(name) returning a dict or None, update_item(name, quantity=None, price=None) raising KeyError if not found, delete_item(name) raising KeyError if not found, and list_items() returning a list of all item dicts. Do not include explanations; provide only the code.

tasks.description: Implement an LRU cache using a dictionary and deque.
tasks.input_specification: Constructor accepts capacity (int); supports get(key) and put(key, value)
tasks.output_specification: An LRUCache class; get returns value or -1 if absent; put evicts LRU item when capacity exceeded
tasks.prompt: Write a Python class to implement an LRU (Least Recently Used) cache using a dictionary and collections.deque. The class LRUCache must accept capacity in its constructor and support get(key) returning the value or -1 if absent, and put(key, value) which inserts the key-value pair and evicts the least recently used item if capacity is exceeded. Do not include explanations; provide only the code.
